item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I fixed that. Thank you for letting me notice,

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'll just hang onto this SCP and continue checking on it for a while if it stays fully afloat..





already tagged, anyways…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Possibly. I've seen it in all other articles with MTFs that I've read so far, so maybe.
Not to mention the 3rd paragraph in the Special Containment Procedures where it's striked out mentions it with quotation marks instead of parenthesis.

MTF Kilo-19, designated “Cat Eyes”

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Should MTF nicknames be in quotation marks?

On ██/██/201█, MTF Kilo-19 (Cat Eyes) was mobilized by Site-77's

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Staff notice: this user has been permanently banned for plagiarism.

item.title: Re: Rieview
item.content:encoded: Thank you for the review. I'll try to work on the leaps that my article does not explain. I can't think of anything to make the prose "easier" to read. Thank you again. I'll start fixing the errors you made me noticed tomorrow morning.

item.title: Rieview
item.content:encoded:
This is very odd.

There's a lot going on here. I actually get the sense that the author has an explanation for everything and a very clear picture for both atmosphere and story. And the prose is juuuust on the edge of being well written - it's clear that the understanding is there, but the execution is just slightly off.

That being said. This article is really hurt by two things. There are a bunch of leaps that are never explained: things like this,

During SCP-5139's dormant phase, previously formed SCP-5139-1 instances are reported to still exist;

that really catch me off guard and come out of the blue. The thing is, this is the same article that utilises that technique of introducing unknown information well:

Following Incident-5139-1-A, the Special Containment Procedures were updated: multiple power units have been implemented to ensure the correct functionality of the Thanatos Server

This is a great example of introducing something that the reader doesn't know but the universe does. I have no idea what Incident-5139-1-A is, but I now understand its place in the narrative.

The second thing is that this drags. There's so much and the prose really slows me down.

I'm not sure what I'd recommend here. I don't think anything short of a major rewrite with another author good fix the problems I've outlined here.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thank you for letting me notice. The typos will be correct in 24 hours.

I corrected the typos. Thank you again.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: There are a lot of SPaG issues in this article, I think you should go through it (or have someone do so) to look for things to fix.

item.title: Acheron's Mists/SCP-5139
item.content:encoded:
My first article:

Draft Critique Forum: http://www.scp-wiki.net/forum/t-13280887

Thanks to barredowl and sonderance for helping with the draft

Greenlighted by nagiros and cybersqyd

PAGE SOURCE:

Link: https://unsplash.com/photos/jWLLzJ7S2tc by Jamie Hagan
